headline: Walmart raises the red flag on rising prices
summary: Oil prices impact not just what you pay to gas up your car or heat your house; they impact everything you buy. "Even modest increases in oil prices can gradually push up the cost of everyday goods and services," according to Kiplinger. Gas prices tend to be the first place consumers notice rising ...

headline: Where Will Walmart Stock Be By 2030?
summary: Walmart (NYSE:WMT) just delivered a quarter that makes the bull case for 2030 hard to ignore. Q1 FY27 revenue hit $175.68 billion, up 6.08% YoY, with global eCommerce climbing 26% and advertising revenue jumping 37%. New CEO John Furner is leaning into automation and higher-margin commerce. Yet shares are down 8.39% in the past week ... Where Will Walmart Stock Be By 2030?

headline: Walmart Is A Great Retailer 'Priced For Perfection'— But Former US CEO Bill Simon Says He'd Rather Own Target Stock: Here's Why
summary: Bill Simon, Former CEO of Walmart U.S., weighed in on Walmart Inc. first-quarter results and its stock valuation. Simon commended the company’s digital sales growth in its recent quarter, despite the economic challenges presented by high gas prices. Speaking on...

headline: Retailers look to bridge the K-shaped economy with dual playbooks of price cuts and premiumization
summary: As the K-shaped economy continues to divide Americans, retailers are courting lower-income customers with lower prices while catering to more affluent customers with premium offerings.

raw.headline: Retailers look to bridge the K-shaped economy with dual playbooks of price cuts and premiumization
raw.summary: As the K-shaped economy continues to divide Americans, retailers are courting lower-income customers with lower prices while catering to more affluent customers with premium offerings.

headline: WMT Q1 Deep Dive: Strong Sales Growth Overshadowed by Cautious Guidance and Margin Pressures
summary: Retail behemoth Walmart (NASDAQ:WMT) reported Q1 CY2026 results exceeding the market’s revenue expectations, with sales up 7.3% year on year to $177.8 billion. On the other hand, next quarter’s revenue guidance of $185.4 billion was less impressive, coming in 0.5% below analysts’ estimates. Its non-GAAP profit of $0.66 per share was in line with analysts’ consensus estimates.

headline: Walmart (WMT) Stock Trades Down, Here Is Why
summary: Shares of retail behemoth Walmart (NASDAQ:WMT) fell 6.8% in the afternoon session after the company reported a weaker-than-expected earnings outlook, which overshadowed its solid first-quarter results.
